meta.name: Chicken Enchiladas Rojas
meta.subtitle: Corn-tortilla enchiladas baked in a mild red chile sauce with optional cheese

summary: Classic baked enchiladas rojas with a simple red chile sauce and a flexible chicken filling. Designed for SSA planning: batch-friendly, scales well, and reheats beautifully.

yield.notes: Makes about 12 enchiladas. Adjust tortilla count for larger pans.

ingredients.label: Chili powder
ingredients.notes: Or use a mix of guajillo/ancho for deeper flavor

steps.title: Preheat and prep pan
steps.body: Heat oven to 375°F (190°C). Lightly oil a 9x13-inch baking dish.

steps.title: Make quick red enchilada sauce
steps.body: In a saucepan, heat 1 tbsp oil over medium heat. Sauté half the onion (about half) until soft, then add half the garlic and cook 30 seconds. Stir in chili powder (and tomato paste if using) for 30–60 seconds. Whisk in broth gradually. Simmer 5–7 minutes until slightly thickened; season with salt and a small splash of vinegar/lime if desired.
checks: Chili powder is bloomed (no raw taste)

steps.title: Season the chicken filling
steps.body: In a skillet, heat 1 tbsp oil over medium heat. Sauté remaining onion until soft, add remaining garlic for 30 seconds. Add shredded chicken, cumin, Mexican oregano, salt, and pepper. Stir until hot and well seasoned. If dry, add a splash of broth or a few spoonfuls of sauce.
checks: Filling tastes seasoned on its own

steps.title: Soften tortillas
steps.body: Warm tortillas briefly so they bend without cracking. Option A: heat on a dry comal 15–20 seconds per side. Option B: lightly oil a skillet and warm each tortilla quickly. Keep covered so they stay pliable.

steps.title: Assemble enchiladas
steps.body: Spread a thin layer of sauce in the bottom of the baking dish. Fill each tortilla with chicken, roll tightly, and place seam-side down. Pour remaining sauce over the top. Add cheese if using.

steps.title: Bake
steps.body: Bake 18–22 minutes until bubbling and heated through. If using cheese, it should be melted and lightly browned at edges.

steps.title: Rest and serve
steps.body: Rest 5 minutes. Serve with cilantro and lime wedges. Optional sides: arroz rojo, beans, salad.

variations.label: Enchiladas Verdes
variations.notes: Swap the red sauce for salsa verde (tomatillo-based). Keep filling the same or add sautéed poblano.

variations.label: Cheese & Bean Enchiladas
variations.notes: Replace chicken with a mix of refried beans and cheese (or dairy-free cheese).

variations.label: Dairy-Free
variations.notes: Omit cheese or use a dairy-free substitute. Add avocado slices or extra salsa for richness.

variations.label: Spicier Rojas
variations.notes: Add chipotle in adobo or a pinch of cayenne to the sauce; garnish with sliced jalapeño.

storage.refrigeration: Cool and refrigerate covered for up to 4 days.
storage.freezing: Freeze fully assembled (unbaked or baked) for up to 2 months. Thaw overnight before baking/reheating.
storage.reheat: Reheat covered at 350°F (175°C) until hot; add a splash of broth/sauce if dry.

notes: Cool cooked enchiladas within 2 hours before refrigerating.
Reheat leftovers to steaming hot throughout.

planner.leftoversPlan: Portion into single-meal containers with rice/beans for grab-and-go.

notes: Canonical SSA enchiladas baseline: quick sauce + flexible filling + bake.
If you maintain a strict corn-only policy, ensure chili powder is gluten-free (some blends contain anti-caking agents).
For deeper flavor, replace chili powder with toasted dried guajillo/ancho chiles blended with broth, then simmer.
